methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Western Chemicals operates in the Climate & Energy sector at the stealth stage. The comparable universe comprises 9 peers across public and private markets: 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private firms including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

The peer-median valuation method is not applicable to this analysis. While 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, the filtering criteria—same stage, adjacent stage, and similar valuation tier—yielded fewer than three datapoints in any single category. Applying an all-sector median would introduce uncontrolled heterogeneity and produce a misleading result. The sparse overlap between Western Chemicals' stealth-stage profile and the available comparable set precludes reliable triangulation via traditional multiples-based approaches.

The current dataset does not support a quantified valuation range. Forward progress on a preliminary valuation would require either (1) expansion of the comparable set to include additional stealth or seed-stage climate technology firms with disclosed metrics, or (2) introduction of alternative methods such as DCF analysis or venture-specific frameworks (e.g., Berkus, scorecard) paired with management guidance on capital efficiency and technical milestones. The key uncertainty remains the absence of sufficient peer data at Western Chemicals' stage within its sector.
